assertion_results.evidence: Final outputs/slugify.ts (ordinal 17) still contains all three restatement one-liners: '// lowercase the title' (line 9), '// replace runs of non-alphanumeric characters with a single hyphen' (line 15), and '// strip leading and trailing hyphens' (line 18). The agent only removed the TICKET-4821 block and tightened the NFKD comment.

Final output (ordinal 17) shows the exported JSDoc remains and was enriched: '* Lowercases, strips accents, and collapses any run of non-alphanumeric / * characters into a single hyphen. Used for blog post URLs to ensure / * consistent routing regardless of title casing or punctuation.' The NFKD kernel was tightened to one line ('// Decompose accents and strip combining marks (NFKD normalization)') but retained — satisfying the 'either may be tightened' allowance.

The agent surfaced integration options without merging: 'The code is reviewed and verified. You can now: Open a PR with this cleaned version / Merge locally if this is going straight to main / Leave it as-is and continue with other work / Discard if you've changed your mind.' No merge, push, or PR tool invocations appear in the run record.

meta_results.evidence: The final_message says 'The code is reviewed and verified' then surfaces integration options ('Open a PR', 'Merge locally', 'Leave it as-is', 'Discard') without choosing — this mirrors the skill's Phase 3 language exactly ('state that the work is reviewed and verified, then offer the user choices such as merge, push/open PR, leave as-is, or discard'). The agent also ran tests after making edits (ordinal 16) and presented the output as evidence before claiming success, matching the Gate Function / Phase 2 sequence.
